signature: const char * ndpi_strncasestr(const char *str1, const char *str2, size_t len)
params: str1 - haystack buffer to search; only `len` bytes are valid (not guaranteed NUL-terminated)
str2 - NUL-terminated needle string to search for (case-insensitive)
len  - number of valid bytes available in str1 (bound passed to strnlen)
harness_callchain: LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput (fuzz/fuzz_ndpi_reader.c) writes the input bytes to a temp pcap, opens it with pcap_open_offline, then for each packet calls ndpi_workflow_process_packet -> ndpi_detection_process_packet -> the HTTP dissector (ndpi_search_http_tcp / check_content_type_and_change_protocol in src/lib/protocols/http.c). When an HTTP 'Authorization:' header is present, the dissector calls ndpi_strncasestr(packet->authorization_line.ptr, "Basic"/"Digest", packet->authorization_line.len). A header value shorter than the 5/6-char needle makes str1_len < str2_len, triggering the unsigned underflow and the OOB read. CAVEAT: the vulnerable ndpi_strncasestr body is present verbatim at tag 4.2, but this specific reachable caller (authorization_line via ndpi_strncasestr) was added by commit 0dd5ac89f on 2022-02-03, two days AFTER the 4.2 tag (2022-02-01); at exactly 4.2 the function has no in-tree caller. The oss-fuzz crash 44482 was reported/fixed on master shortly after 4.2 (fix dated 2022-02-09).
